output: Implemented a narrow Sprint 2b metadata fix and captured execution evidence. The feedback list tools now register as read tools, with direct regression assertions.
commands_run: PYENV_VERSION=3.11.11 python --version && PYENV_VERSION=3.11.11 python -m pytest tests/test_editorial_polish_tools.py tests/test_end_of_turn.py -q

PYENV_VERSION=3.11.11 python -m pytest tests/test_anthropic_model.py tests/test_anthropic_replay.py tests/test_run_turn.py -q
deviations: Default pytest uses Python 3.8.10 and fails during collection because the repo imports datetime.UTC. Verification was run with PYENV_VERSION=3.11.11, which matches the codebase requirement.

The full Python 3.11 suite still fails in tests/test_no_leaked_secrets.py because existing .megaplan sprint-1b artifacts contain a Supabase JWT prefix. I did not edit those unrelated generated artifacts.
A requested checkpoint file was not present at .megaplan/plans/sprint-2b-editorial-polish/execution_checkpoint.json, so no checkpoint update was written.
The temporary reproduction script tmp_verify_sprint2b_tool_kinds.py was created, run successfully, and deleted before final output.

After-execute Discord smoke testing remains manual: explicit feedback save, proposed feedback confirmation, hot-context reload, observation recording/resolution, and default acknowledgment must be checked in the user's resident Discord runtime after deployment/migration.
